返回本批次

GitHub 现在有问题

GitHub is having issues now

www.githubstatus.com 作者 SenHeng 分数 317 评论 116 2026/04/28 01:42
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 已解决 - 2026 年 4 月 20 日 10:28 UTC 至 15:04 UTC 期间，GitHub 遇到了代码扫描默认设置、代码质量和项目板服务降级的情况。受影响项目板的修复还持续到 UTC 时间 4 月 21 日 05:04 在此期间，新打开的拉取请求不会触发代码扫描默认设置和代码质量分析。此外，新创建的问题没有出现在项目板上。原因是序列化错误导致无法正确触发代码扫描、代码质量分析和项目板更新。我们通过部署修复程序、恢复代码扫描和代码质量的事件发布来缓解该问题。对于项目板，部署了额外的代码更改来更新事件使用者，然后对受影响的项目项重新建立索引。我们正在努力通过加强模式验证和改进对关键水电主题发布下降的监控来防止再次发生。
  2. UTC 时间 4 月 21 日 05:04 更新 - 降级已得到缓解。我们正在监控以确保稳定性。
  3. UTC 时间 4 月 21 日 04:18 更新 - 该问题仍然得到缓解。当我们完成重新索引时，事件期间与项目相关的问题可能还需要大约三个小时才能正确呈现。
  4. UTC 时间 4 月 21 日 03:10 更新 - 降级已得到缓解。我们正在监控以确保稳定性。
  5. UTC 时间 4 月 20 日 21:36 更新 - 降级已得到缓解。我们正在监控以确保稳定性。

Original Summary

  1. Resolved - On April 20, 2026 between 10:28 UTC and 15:04 UTC GitHub experienced degraded service for code scanning default setup, code quality, and project boards. Repair of affected project boards additionally lasted until April 21, 05:04 UTC During this time, code scanning default setup and code quality analyses were not triggered on newly opened pull requests. Additionally, newly created issues were not appearing on project boards. The cause was a serialization error that prevented proper triggering of code scanning, code quality analyses, and project board updates. We mitigated the issue by deploying a fix, restoring event publishing for code scanning and code quality. For project boards, an additional code change was deployed to update event consumers, followed by a reindex of affected project items. We are working to prevent recurrence by strengthening our schema validations and improving monitoring for drops in publishing on critical hydro topics.
  2. Apr 21, 05:04 UTC Update - The degradation has been mitigated. We are monitoring to ensure stability.
  3. Apr 21, 04:18 UTC Update - The issue remains mitigated. Issues that were linked to projects during the incident may take approximately three more hours to render correctly while we complete a re-index.
  4. Apr 21, 03:10 UTC Update - The degradation has been mitigated. We are monitoring to ensure stability.
  5. Apr 20, 21:36 UTC Update - The degradation has been mitigated. We are monitoring to ensure stability.

评论（默认中文）

cromka 2026/04/28 02:22
根据记录，它也在悄然失败，例如“没有任何开放的拉取请求。”尽管有几十个。那就太糟糕了，这肯定会误导人们。
For the record, it's failing silently, too, showing e.g. "There aren’t any open pull requests." even though there are dozens. That's pretty bad, this will definitely mislead people.
在 HN 中查看
dclowd9901 2026/04/28 02:58
或者上周的“如果你使用合并队列，哎呀，我们不小心破坏了你的主干”，这也悄无声息地失败了。
Or last week's "If you use merge queue, oopsie, we accidentally destroyed your trunk", which also failed silently.
在 HN 中查看
scottbez1 2026/04/28 03:36
令我惊讶的是，该事件似乎没有引起太多关注，因为这是一个相当大的数据损坏错误，但我想与许多此类可用性问题相比，受影响的存储库/客户的范围要小得多？
I was surprised that incident didn’t seem to get as much attention since that was a pretty major data corruption bug, but I guess it was a much smaller scope of impacted repos/customers than a lot of these availability issues?
在 HN 中查看
dclowd9901 2026/04/28 06:23
不用伸手去拿我的锡纸帽子，我就有一种感觉，MS 能够以某种方式抑制这些事件，因为是的，那件事非常糟糕。
Without reaching for my tin foil hat, I have a feeling MS is able to suppress these incidents somehow, because yeah, that one was pretty bad.
在 HN 中查看
bonesss 2026/04/28 14:36
我把脸包在锡箔纸上：在亚马逊、微软、GitHub、Anthropic 和 OpenAI 上，我看到了很多顶级服务问题，听起来很像尚未经过充分测试的代码就影响了生产。

Breaking buttons on the website is one thing, kinda, but Enterprise used to mean a certain degree of robustness and seriousness in product management.

Wrapping my face in tinfoil: across the board, Amazon, Microsoft, GitHub, Anthropic and OpenAI, I’m seeing a lot of top-level service issues that sound an awful lot like code hitting production that hasn’t been fully tested.

Breaking buttons on the website is one thing, kinda, but Enterprise used to mean a certain degree of robustness and seriousness in product management.

在 HN 中查看
serial_dev 2026/04/28 16:51
开发人员预计交付速度将加快 10 倍。人工智能工具确实有一点帮助，比如可能是 2 倍，但 10 倍的“改进”来自于除了交付分配的功能之外不考虑任何其他事情，没有仔细测试你的代码，没有得到适当的代码审查，没有对你的东西进行测试，并且不小心发布。
Devs are expected to ship slop 10x faster. The AI tools genuinely help a bit, like maybe 2x, but the 10x "improvement" comes from not thinking about anything else than shipping your assigned features, not testing your code carefully, not getting proper code reviews, not dogfooding your stuff, and releasing carelessly.
在 HN 中查看
MarkMarine 2026/04/28 12:38
在我工作的产品中断期间肯定引起了注意。我要寻找另一种选择，我厌倦了这种糟糕的正常运行时间。
Definitely got attention during the prod outage at my work. I’m going to find another alternative, I’m sick of this terrible uptime.
在 HN 中查看
yearolinuxdsktp 2026/04/28 06:31
合并队列并不常用……4 小时内大约有 2000 个 PR 受到影响。我认为每个租户大约需要 10 次提交。这是一个吸引力较低的功能，可能是因为它产生的问题多于它解决的问题。
Merge queues are not as frequently used… ~2000 PRs affected over 4 hours. I reckon that’s on the order of 10 commits per tenant. It’s a feature with low traction, probably because it creates more problems than it solves.
在 HN 中查看
move-on-by 2026/04/28 11:27
合并队列很棒。

该错误仅影响使用合并队列和压缩/变基合并（而不是默认合并提交）的存储库

Merge queues are great.

The bug only affected repos using merge queues AND squash/rebase merging (instead of the default merge commit)

在 HN 中查看
elischleifer 2026/04/28 03:17
我们碰巧构建了完美的解决方案来避免 GitHub 合并队列 - 我们的:)

它的性能也大大提高

https://trunk.io/merge-queue

We happen to build the perfect solution to avoiding GitHubs Merge Queue - ours :)

It’s also massively more performant

https://trunk.io/merge-queue

在 HN 中查看
rileymichael 2026/04/28 04:21
虽然外部合并队列提供了更多的功能，但基于用户体验固定的简单事实，我不会将它们中的任何一个描述为“完美”。 github 继续显示其本机 UI 组件进行合并，用户被迫通过注释或外部 CLI/网页中的晦涩命令进行交互。不理想！
while external merge queues offer a ton more features, i wouldn't describe any of them as 'perfect' based on the simple fact the UX is bolted on. github continues to display their native UI components for merging, and users are forced to interact via arcane commands in comments or external CLIs/webpages. not ideal!
在 HN 中查看
techterrier 2026/04/28 02:55
为自己说话，我们正在庆祝已完成所有 PR 的更改：D
speak for yourself, we are celebrating having completed all our PR's for a change :D
在 HN 中查看
enraged_camel 2026/04/28 03:46
即使它确实显示了 PR 列表，它也不一定显示 全部 正在查看的类别中的 PR。真是令人讨厌的问题。
Even when it does show the PR list, it doest necessarily show all the PRs in the category being viewed. Truly nasty issue.
在 HN 中查看
yallpendantools 2026/04/28 05:42
伙计，这对我来说太糟糕了。就在几个月前，出于协同效应和效率的原因，$PARENT_CONGLOMERATE 强制其仁慈翼下的所有人员迁移到 GitHub。现在轮到我从 $DAYJOB 迁移我们的自托管 Gitlab 实例了。我已经有一些委屈了……

- 围绕 GH 帐户的 IT 政策毫无意义。这是一个很长的故事，但简而言之，您不能使用任何现有的 GH 帐户，无论是个人帐户还是专业帐户（例如，我在 Synergy Mandate 之前专门为 $DAYJOB 创建的帐户）并且 必须 创建一个符合 IT 惯例的新方案。

- 我们不单一存储库，因此我们广泛使用组。 GitHub 中没有这个概念的直接映射，因此我们必须手动命名项目。

- 现在当然 GH 的无九可用性:(

对于我的团队来说，利润恰好对我们的发布日期很敏感——如果我们是否做出本月的预测，一两天的延迟确实会产生影响。在另一个世界中，我会主动镜像我们的利润基本代码，但不值得冒险进行臭鼬工厂的游击努力。我想我们可以在不久的将来在一些事后分析中归咎于协同指令，但当然我昨天没有毕业，我知道这不会发生。

我们不断地思考和祈祷我们的利润预测，他们不会因为表现不佳而贬低我们的产品。

（写下来，我真切地感受到这份工作自从我加入以来发生了怎样的变化。）

Man, this SUCKS big time for me. Just a few months ago, $PARENT_CONGLOMERATE mandated all under its benevolent wing to migrate to GitHub for reasons of synergy and efficiency. So now it's my turn at $DAYJOB to be migrating us from our self-hosted Gitlab instance. I already have a few grievances...

- IT policies around GH accounts make no sense. It's a long story but, in short, you can't use any of your pre-existing GH accounts whether personal or professional (as in, an account I made exclusively for $DAYJOB before The Synergy Mandate) and must create a new one aligned with IT conventions.

- We don't monorepo hence we made extensive use of groups. There is no direct mapping for this concept in GitHub so we have to manually namespace projects.

- And now of course GH's no-nines availability :(

For my team, profit happens to be sensitive to our release dates---a day or two of delay can really make the difference if we'll make the month's projections or not. In another world, I would proactively mirror our profit-essential code but it's not worth the risk making a skunkworks guerilla effort. I'd like to think we can blame The Synergy Mandate in a few postmortems in the near future but of course I did not graduate yesterday, I know that's not gonna happen.

Thoughts and prayers we keep hitting our profit projections and they don't axe our product for underperformance.

(Writing this down, I can really feel how this job has changed since I joined.)

在 HN 中查看
Arcuru 2026/04/28 02:51
提醒所有 OSS 项目：设置一个简单的 CI 作业以保持多个 Forge 之间的代码同步非常容易。从第二个 Forge 获取电子邮件通知是 0 额外的努力。

至少让人们可以选择开始离开 GitHub 来为你的项目做出贡献。最终，这将对生态系统更好。

Reminder to all OSS projects: it is extraordinarily easy to setup a simple CI job to keep your code in sync between multiple Forges. And getting email notifications from a second Forge is 0 extra effort.

At least give people the option to start moving away from GitHub to contribute to your project. It will, ultimately, be better for the ecosystem.

在 HN 中查看
kakwa_ 2026/04/28 03:22
同步代码是简单而琐碎的部分，您的 CI 工作只是解决这个问题。在我看来，对于大多数项目来说这甚至没有必要。

困难的部分是代码周围的所有内容：

* 门票/PR（包括已关闭的）

* 引用项目的链接

* CI 设置

* 对于大型项目，提交者权限设置

* 如果适用，推送/提交/分支规则

所有这些在每个项目的基础上迁移都会非常烦人，或者可能会丢失。

但在我看来，这还不是最糟糕的。失去寻找软件的首选平台是（软件何时变得多样化？）。

Syncing the code is the easy and trivial part and your CI job is only solving that. And in my opinion, it's not even that necessary for most projects.

The difficult part is all what's around the code:

* the tickets/PR (including the closed ones)

* the links referencing the project

* the CI setup

* for large projects, the committers permission setup

* if applicable, the push/commit/branch rules

All that will be deeply annoying to migrate on a per project basis, or might get lost.

But that's not even the worst on my opinion. Losing the go-to platform for finding software is (fediverse for software when?).

在 HN 中查看
rurban 2026/04/28 15:01
同步很简单，CI 才是重点。 GH 行动仍然是最好的选择。无论是 FSF 还是任何其他 OSS 实验室都没有为我们开源维护者提供合适的 CI。此后 CI 负载也大幅增加。
Syncing is trivial, the CI is the deal. GH actions are the best option still. Neither the FSF nor any other OSS lab didn't come up with a proper CI for us open source maintainers. The CI load also increased massively since.
在 HN 中查看
djyde 2026/04/28 04:20
设置自己的 GitLab 实例也可能是一个很好的解决方案。
Setting up own GitLab instance might be a good solution too.
在 HN 中查看
agartner 2026/04/28 02:23
是的，我想我终于受够了。我需要开始认真倡导替代方案，因为这开始影响我们的业务。显然情况并没有好转。
Yeah I think I've finally had enough. I need to start seriously advocating for alternatives since this is starting to impact our business. It's clearly not getting any better.
在 HN 中查看
rhdunn 2026/04/28 03:00
如果您想要类似 GitHub 的 UI（具有 org/repo 结构限制），请使用 Forgejo 或 Gitea。

如果您想要类似但不同的体验，请使用 GitLab。

如果您想要更类似于内核体验的东西（即托管、灵活的存储库结构、通过 ssh 密钥进行用户身份验证以及简单的 Web UI），请使用 gitolite 和 cgit，或者使用 gitweb。

If you want a GitHub-like UI (with org/repo structure limitations) use either Forgejo or Gitea.

If you want a similar but different experience use GitLab.

If you want something more akin to the kernel experience (i.e. hosting, flexible repository structure, user auth via ssh keys, and a simple web UI) use gitolite with cgit, or alternatively gitweb.

在 HN 中查看
dymk 2026/04/28 10:21
我喜欢 gitea，并且将它用于我的家庭实验室，但权限系统需要大量工作。仍然存在一个未解决的错误，无论设置如何，除了存储库所有者之外的任何人都不允许读取 CI 日志。
I love gitea, and I use it for my homelab, but the permissions system needs a lot of work. There’s still an open bug which doesn’t let anyone but the repo owner read CI logs regardless of settings.
在 HN 中查看
rhdunn 2026/04/28 15:45
我使用过 Gitea 一段时间，但最终改用了 gitolite+cgit。这是由于 org/repo 结构不适合我的 git 层次结构（我正在使用主题/repo、主题/子主题/repo 样式结构）以及缺乏组织/主题范围的问题跟踪/管理。
I used Gitea for a while, but eventually switched to gitolite+cgit. That was down to the org/repo structure not fitting my git hierarchy (I'm using a topic/repo, topic/subtopic/repo style structure) and the lack of organization/topic wide issue tracking/management.
在 HN 中查看
dijit 2026/04/28 03:20
总是有格里特。

我的意思是，从技术上讲，这是一个 代码审查 平台，不是像Gitlab之类的完整工具箱，但如果不是最专业的感觉体验该死的。

There's always gerrit.

I mean, technically it's a code review platform, not a complete toolbox like Gitlab and co, but damn if it isn't the most professional feeling experience.

在 HN 中查看
mghackerlady 2026/04/28 05:34
或 sr.ht，如果您愿意，您可以自己托管
or sr.ht, you can host it yourself if you want
在 HN 中查看
homebrewer 2026/04/28 02:30
前进。多年来，我们一直在使用 Drone/Woodpecker 自行托管 Gitea；如果您对它们的功能集感到满意的话，它或 Forgejo 都可以。有时我会走进这些 GitHub 线程来开怀大笑；我们的 Gitea 实例在过去几年中总共出现了几分钟的停机时间，所有这些停机时间都是计划好的（升级 Gitea）并且发生在半夜。
Go ahead. We've been self-hosting Gitea with Drone/Woodpecker for years; either it or Forgejo will do fine if you're okay with their feature set. I sometimes wander into these GitHub threads to have a laugh; our Gitea instance has had several minutes of downtime combined over the last few years, all of them planned (to upgrade Gitea) and in the middle of the night.
在 HN 中查看
lioeters 2026/04/28 04:30
哦，啄木鸟 CI 与 Gitea 合作 和 锻造 https://woodpecker-ci.org/ 这可能是我将 Git 存储库从 GitHub 迁移到自托管 forge 所需的最后一部分。

编辑：实际上有 Gitea Actions 和 Forgejo Actions，这可能足以满足我的用例。

https://docs.gitea.com/usage/actions/

https://forgejo.org/docs/next/user/actions/reference/

Ooh, Woodpecker CI works with Gitea and Forgejo. https://woodpecker-ci.org/ That might be last piece I needed to migrating Git repos from GitHub to a self-hosted forge.

Edit: Actually there's Gitea Actions and Forgejo Actions, that might be enough for my use case.

https://docs.gitea.com/usage/actions/

https://forgejo.org/docs/next/user/actions/reference/

在 HN 中查看
dymk 2026/04/28 10:23
我发现 gitea actions（基于 ACT，因此它几乎与 GitHub action runner 相同）非常有效。迁移 GitHub 工作流程主要只是更改文件名。
I’ve found gitea actions (based on ACT, so it’s nearly identical to a GitHub action runner) to work great. Migrating a GitHub workflow is mostly just a file name change.
在 HN 中查看
lioeters 2026/04/28 10:49
很高兴知道！
Good to know!
在 HN 中查看
MiracleRabbit 2026/04/28 02:53
Gitea 升级..替换二进制文件，重新启动。我喜欢它。

福尔赫霍也一样。

Gitea Upgrading.. replacing binary, restarting. I love it.

Same for Forgejo.

在 HN 中查看
scottyah 2026/04/28 04:01
我在 Woodpecker 上挣扎了一段时间，但现在 gitea 的 Actions 非常适合我的用例（并且少了一个支持工具）。我相信他们还强调了与 github 操作协议之类的兼容性。可能值得研究一下。
I struggled with Woodpecker for a bit, but now gitea has Actions that work wonderfully for my use case (and one less tool to support). I believe they also highlight compatibility with a github action protocol of sorts. Might be worth looking into.

1970-01-01 2026/04/28 03:26
我很惊讶 GitLab 没有得到更多关注。是的，它不是抄本，但已经很接近了。苹果和梨代替橙子。
I'm surprised GitLab isn't getting more attention. Yes, its not a carbon copy, but it is close. Apples and pears instead of oranges.

BoingBoomTschak 2026/04/28 04:14
从某种意义上说，它很接近，它也是 Jabbascript SPA 垃圾，需要一台超级计算机才能（尝试但失败）显示几千行的差异，你的意思是？我们在工作中使用它，但它非常糟糕。
It's close in the sense that it's also Jabbascript SPA crap that needs a supercomputer just to (try and fail to) display a diff of a few thousands lines, you mean? We're using it at work and it sucks massively.

ahartmetz 2026/04/28 14:54
我宁愿在我自己的服务器（即 GitLab）上运行开放核心，但性能与 Git**b 可接受的性能相差几个数量级。
I'd rather have the open core one running on my own servers (i.e. GitLab), but performance is a few orders of magnitude away from acceptable for both Git**b.

richstokes 2026/04/28 05:15
老实说，我也这么想。不过，GH 非常具有粘性，尤其是当您设置了操作和各种其他集成时。但目前来看他们有多少次停电有点荒谬。
Was thinking the same honestly. GH is very sticky though, especially when you have actions and all kinds of other integrations set up. But it’s just kind of absurd at this point how many outages they have.

TacticalCoder 2026/04/28 05:16
几乎就好像微软接触过的任何东西都像 Windows 一样有缺陷，同时又像 Excel 一样变得不必要的复杂（和滥用）。

等不及微软走 IBM 的路了。

It's nearly as if anything touched by Microsoft was tending to be as buggy as Windows while getting as needlessly complex (and misused) as Excel.

Can't wait for Microsoft to go the IBM way.

rurban 2026/04/28 14:58
自从他们启动 GitHub Actions 以来，它就是 Azure 的测试平台。由于 Azure 管理不善且注定失败 https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47616242 此类中断是正常的。我认为他们雇佣了廉价的员工来按需手动修复过度配置的盒子。微软工作质量
It's a testbed for Azure since they started GitHub Actions. Since Azure is mismanaged and doomed https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47616242 such outages are normal. I think they hired cheap staff to manually fix overprovisioned boxes on demand. Microsoft Quality at work
在 HN 中查看
recitedropper 2026/04/28 02:32
讨厌 GitHub 宕机，又讨厌 AI 窃取你的代码？加入sourcehut——它对我来说非常有用，我很高兴看到它作为一个平台蓬勃发展。
Hate GitHub being down, plus hate AI stealing your code? Join sourcehut--it has worked great for me, and I'd love to see it flourish as a platform.
在 HN 中查看
yrds96 2026/04/28 03:21
我喜欢探索新存储库的体验，因此我将所有内容都切换到了 codeberg，这是我感兴趣的大多数项目所在的位置
I like the experience of exploring new repositories so I switched everything to codeberg which is where most of the projects I'm interested with are
在 HN 中查看
pokstad 2026/04/28 02:57
sourcehut 有何不同？这只是另一个中心化服务。
How is sourcehut different? It’s just yet another centralized service.
在 HN 中查看
recitedropper 2026/04/28 03:07
如果您需要自托管，请自托管。 Sourcehut 显然不能替代它。

但是，如果不是的话：那就不同了，因为德鲁·德瓦特（Drew DeVault）严厉反对人工智能，并且有坚持强烈观点（无论好坏）的历史。如果您担心人工智能抓取和停机时间，这似乎是外部源代码控制的最佳选择。

If you need to self-host, self-host. Sourcehut is obviously not a replacement for that.

But, if not: It is different because Drew DeVault is scathingly anti-AI, and has a history of sticking to strong opinions (for better or worse). Seems like the best bet for off-premise source control if you are concerned about AI scraping and downtime.

在 HN 中查看
Conscat 2026/04/28 03:03
我想至少它不会经常下降。我认为大多数用户确实想要一个集中锻造，为他们提供可发现性和星图。
At least it doesn't go down as often, I guess. I think most users do want a centralized forge that gives them discoverability and star graphs.
在 HN 中查看
arielcostas 2026/04/28 03:04
> 这只是另一个中心化服务

是的，协作通常需要某种集中化。无论是 LKML+git.kernel.org、gitlab.gnome.org、salsa.debian.org 或 Sourcehut，还是 GitHub。至少 Sourcehut 并不是完全专有的，它会利用一切可能的机会将人工智能强加给你。对于 Codeberg 和几乎所有 GitLab CE、Gitea 或 Forgejo 实例来说也是如此

> It’s just yet another centralized service

Yeah, collaboration usually requires some sort of centralisation. Whether that is the LKML+git.kernel.org, gitlab.gnome.org, salsa.debian.org or Sourcehut, or GitHub. At least Sourcehut isn't completely proprietary and shoving AI down your throat at every possible chance. The same can be said for Codeberg and almost any GitLab CE, Gitea or Forgejo instance

在 HN 中查看
senko 2026/04/28 05:35
如果我讨厌 github 宕机但喜欢 AI 窃取我的代码，我有什么选择？
what are my options if I hate github being down but love AI stealing my code?
在 HN 中查看
[deleted] 2026/04/28 09:34 deleted

[deleted]

在 HN 中查看
lrvick 2026/04/28 03:32
每次 Github 崩溃时，就会有更多的人转向道德替代方案，并减少 FOSS 社区在 Microsoft 中出现的 SPOF。

https://sfconservancy.org/GiveUpGitHub/

Every time Github goes down, a few more people move to ethical alternatives and reduce the FOSS community having a SPOF in Microsoft.

https://sfconservancy.org/GiveUpGitHub/

在 HN 中查看
tracker1 2026/04/28 04:10
虽然我很欣赏这种情绪……但在协作方面，GitHub 上的许多/大多数项目的社交方面都有一些好处。 我认为出于多种原因，开始出现很多摩擦。 我已经看到越来越多的问题被用作垃圾邮件，更不用说更多的邪恶活动了。
While I appreciate the sentiment... there was something nice about the social aspects of many/most projects being on GitHub in terms of collaboration. I think there is starting to be a lot of friction for many reasons. I've been seeing more use of issues as spam, not to mention even more nefarious activities making rounds.
在 HN 中查看
lrvick 2026/04/28 08:21
Corpos 往往位于 Github 上，其中以社区为中心的项目正在非常迅速地转移到 codeberg，它是完全开源的，并且还具有实际有效的提交签名集成。
Corpos tend to be on Github, where community focused projects are very rapidly shifting to codeberg, which is fully open source and also has commit signing integration that actually works.
在 HN 中查看
lioeters 2026/04/28 04:33
SPOF = 单点故障
SPOF = Single Point of Failure
在 HN 中查看
iLemming 2026/04/28 03:49
哇，修复这个问题需要很长时间。我想试图修复它的团队已经达到了克劳德的会话限制，现在在冷却时间结束之前什么也做不了，唯一知道如何在没有人工智能的情况下修复它的人已经出去接受手术了。当整整一代知道如何在不使用人工智能的情况下解决问题的人退休时，会发生什么？
Wow, this taking unusually long to fix. I suppose the team trying to fix it hit the Claude session limits and now can't do anything until the end of the cooldown and the only person who knows how to fix it without AI is out for a surgery. When the entire generation of people who knew how to fix shit without using AI will retire, what happens then?
在 HN 中查看
swiftcoder 2026/04/28 03:02
> 用户在查看问题、拉取请求、项目和操作工作流程运行时遇到间歇性故障

“间歇性”是一种低估约 9/10 页面加载失败的情况

> Users are experiencing intermittent failures to view issues, pull requests, projects and Actions workflow runs

"intermittent" is kind of underselling a failure on ~9/10 page loads

在 HN 中查看
pier25 2026/04/28 02:25
自微软收购以来，Github 一直存在问题。

https://damrnelson.github.io/github-historical-uptime/

Github has been having issues since the Microsoft acquisition.

https://damrnelson.github.io/github-historical-uptime/

在 HN 中查看
fishtoaster 2026/04/28 03:04
这并不是说事情没有随着时间的推移而变得更糟，但是......

我不认为该图表显示了它看起来所显示的内容。 2018 年之前有很多中断没有出现在此处： https://hn.algolia.com/?dateEnd=1545696000&dateRange=custom&...

该图表的另一种解释是“在微软收购之后，他们开始认真对待实际跟踪中断。”

据说，据说，感觉 很多 过去 6 个月情况更糟。 我猜这是 MS 引起的质量下降和 AI 引起的规模增加的结合。

This is not to say that things haven't gotten worse over time, but...

I don't think that chart shows what it seems like it shows. There were plenty of pre-2018 outages that don't show up there: https://hn.algolia.com/?dateEnd=1545696000&dateRange=custom&...

An alternate interpretation of that chart is "After the microsoft acquisition, they got serious about actually tracking outages."

That said, anecdotally, it's felt much worse over the last 6 months. I'd guess it's a combination of MS-induced quality drops and AI-induced scale increases.

在 HN 中查看
r14c 2026/04/28 04:02
他们要迁移到 Azure，必须首先修复 Azure，使其足够稳定，以便 GH 甚至可以考虑迁移。

我猜测它是 Azure 的组合仍然不够稳定，也是试图将整个公司的运营从物理 DC 转移到云中的副产品。

They're moving to Azure and had to fix up Azure first to be stable enough for GH to even consider moving.

I'm guessing its a combo of Azure still not being stable enough and a byproduct of trying to move an entire company's operations from a physical DC into a cloud while its running.

在 HN 中查看
bonesss 2026/04/28 14:57
远程推测：云是不相称的，大容量云服务将围绕其目标云环境的技术优势/现实来制定关键架构决策。迁移 GitHub 不是一个技术决定，而且基本上是一个愚蠢的想法。

我认为 GitHub 已经远远超过了复杂性的门槛，云开发过程中发生的反思架构无法与产品分开。如果工程师们乞求 Azure，那是一回事，但否则这就是破坏稳定的流失。

我同意 Azure 需要提升才能完成这项工作，并将这种差距视为更根本的挑战。这种变化有点像骨骼移植……管理层的感受和术后愿望并不一定能解释其影响和本质困难。

Speculation from afar: clouds are not commensurate, and high-volume cloud services are going to anchor key architectural decisions around technical benefits/realities of the cloud environment they target. Moving GitHub isn’t a tech decision, and it’s broadly a Dumb Idea.

I think GitHub is well past the complexity threshold where the reflective architecting that happens during cloud development can’t be separated from product. If the Engineers were begging for Azure it’d be one thing, but otherwise this is destabilizing churn.

I agree Azure needed a lift to even handle the job, and see the that gap as indicative of a more fundamental challenge. That change is kinda like a skeleton transplant… managements feelings and post-surgery desires don’t necessarily account for the impact and essential difficulty.

在 HN 中查看
thayne 2026/04/28 04:07
我认为这可能是以下因素的组合：

- 切换到Azure

- 添加更多AI功能

- 更多地利用AI进行开发

- AI代理造成的更高负载

其中三个是微软自上而下的指导。

I think it is probably a combination of:

- Switching to Azure

- Adding more AI features

- Using AI more for development

- Higher load caused by AI agents

Three of those are top-down direction from MS.

在 HN 中查看
shevy-java 2026/04/28 03:32
好吧，也许不是很久以前（例如从收购中），但如果你看看过去四个星期左右，仅这一部分，你可以清楚地看到这里有些东西不起作用。黑客新闻中经常提到微软，但通常不会受到高度赞扬。
Well, perhaps not as long ago (e. g. from the acquisition), but if you look at the last four weeks or so, just that part alone, you can clearly see that something is not working here. Microsoft is constantly mentioned on Hacker News and not typically in a great, praising light.
在 HN 中查看
madeofpalk 2026/04/28 04:00
如果只是过去 4 周，那么我想说微软的收购似乎对他们的可靠性影响不大。

过去 6 个月人工智能的大幅增长给 GitHub 的基础设施带来了巨大压力，这似乎很合理，而且大多数中断都是这样或那样造成的。

If it’s just the last 4 weeks, then I would say it seems the Microsoft acquisition had little impact on their reliability.

It seems pretty reasonable that the massive surge in AI over the past 6 months has put tremendous strain on GitHub’s infrastructure, and most of these outages are as a result of that one way or another.

badeeya 2026/04/28 02:43

wldcordeiro 2026/04/28 02:32
似乎每周左右都会出现状态问题。通常也是在感觉像是一周的开始的时候。
Seems like every week or so there's status issues. Often at what feels like the start of the week too.
在 HN 中查看
adverbly 2026/04/28 04:04
你知道这段时间还有什么变化吗？

他们放弃了 Ruby on Rails。

在我看来，Ruby on Rails 的名声很差。

这可能是互联网时代的缩影，尽管人工智能按比例提高了生产力，但我实际上认为我们还没有达到 PHP 时代刚刚出现的 Rails 巅峰时代的速度、稳定性和简单性。即使在所有这些人工智能削减之后，团队实际上也比现在小得多！

You know what else changed around this time?

They dropped Ruby on Rails.

Ruby on rails got a bad rap IMO.

It was maybe the epitome of the get shit done internet era, and despite AI's proported productivity gains, I actually don't think we've got anywhere close to the velocity, stability, and simplicity of the peak Rails era just coming out of those PHP days. And teams were actually way smaller than they are now even after all these AI cuts!

在 HN 中查看
BoingBoomTschak 2026/04/28 04:08
即使它“有效”，它也没有。看看这个宝石： https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/142308
And even when it's "working", it isn't. See this gem: https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/142308

surgical_fire 2026/04/28 03:50

但在过去一年左右的时间里，确实感觉停电变得司空见惯。

Tbh, for a while GitHub didn't seem to be any more nor less reliable than prior to MS acquiring it.

But in the past year or so, it does feel like outages are becoming commonplace.

dsagent 2026/04/28 02:56

An increasingly disturbing trend from Github and I only see this getting worse.

I wouldn't rule out them moving away from offering the free tier to stop the all the code pushes. I think new code mostly written by AI isn't that appealing of a data set to train on.

tracker1 2026/04/28 03:15
一整天都在注意到这一点...各种工作流程以奇怪的方式失败...奇怪的 UI 问题...实际上推迟了我们的部署一天...太糟糕了，似乎我每个月或更长时间都要修复一次 CI/CD 损坏。
Been noticing this all day.. various workflows failing in weird ways.. strange UI issues... Literally holding off on our deployment for a day... bad enough it seems like I'm fixing a CI/CD breakage once a month or more.

在 HN 中查看
bakies 2026/04/28 02:36
我再次在这里说，自从我部署 gitea 以来，它的正常运行时间更长了。它也更快。自托管万岁。从云端实现多元化，打造您自己的！
I am once again here to say that my gitea has better uptime since I deployed it. It's way snappier too. Long live self-hosting. Diversify from the cloud, build your own!
在 HN 中查看
tomxor 2026/04/28 02:48
另一位快乐的自托管 Gitea 用户已经在这里呆了大约 3 年了。

来自 Gitlab，该公司于 2022 年开始大幅提价，推出基本用户。我权衡了 Github 作为一个选择，但当该服务发生其他一些问题或退化时，我就想“不，我不想在接下来的 5 年内处理同样的问题”。所以这些天我觉得这个决定非常有效。

速度的提升是巨大的（超低延迟），并且值得单独进行切换，但我们还节省了 90% 的直接成本……可能更多的是来自 git 主机的次要影响，只是不是一个痛点。整个 3 年里，我们遇到的唯一一次长时间或计划外停机是 2 小时，当时小型 Linode VPS 主机出现了总体硬件故障并被迁移，对于一个简单、易于托管的单服务器解决方案来说，这是一个非常好的数字 9。我们还获得了 Gitlab 永远无法提供的更持久、更快速的异地备份 (zfs)，但这更多的是自定义的自托管内容，并非特定于 Gitea。

Another happy self hosted Gitea user here for ~3 years now.

Came from Gitlab which started pushing out basic users in 2022 with massive price hikes. I weighed Github as an option but was like "no I don't want to be dealing with this same problem in another 5 years" when some other rug pull or degradation happens with that service. So I'm feeling pretty validated for that decision these days.

The speed improvement was massive (super low latency), and was worth the switch on it's own, but we also saved 90% in immediate cost... probably more in secondary effects from the git host just not being a pain point. The only long or unplanned downtime we've had was 2 hours in that whole 3 years where the tiny Linode VPS host had a total hardware failure and got migrated, which is a pretty damn good number of 9s for a simple easy to host single server solution. We also gained more durable and fast offsite backups (zfs) that Gitlab could never offer, but that's more of a custom self hosted thing not specific to Gitea.

在 HN 中查看
dclowd9901 2026/04/28 03:01

在 HN 中查看
drusepth 2026/04/28 06:53
我不禁想知道 GitHub（以及类似的代码基础设施提供商）是否会在编码代理的 1000 倍或 10000 倍负载下崩溃。
I can't help but wonder if GitHub (and similar code infra providers) are crumbling under 1000x or 10000x load from coding agents.
在 HN 中查看
MerrimanInd 2026/04/28 03:12
疯狂的是，那些最适合去中心化设计的系统，如 git、电子邮件和互联网本身，最终却成为最中心化的、存在单点故障的系统。
It's crazy that the systems the best designed for decentralization like git, email, and the internet itself wound up being the most centralized with single points of failure.
在 HN 中查看
scottyah 2026/04/28 04:04
github 始终是为了方便而选择的 git 反模式。
github was always a git anti-pattern chosen for convenience.

fishgoesblub 2026/04/28 06:24

It's easier, and somehow more reliable to self-host a Gitea instance on consumer hardware than it is to rely on GitHub.

在 HN 中查看
lawn 2026/04/28 04:23
就在今天，我完成了将所有公共和私人存储库迁移到 Forgejo，并将相关公共存储库的推送镜像推送到 Codeberg。

它的敏捷和速度令人难以置信。我极力推荐 Forgejo。

Just today I finished migrated all my public and private repositories to Forgejo, with a push mirror to Codeberg for relevant public repos.

It's unbeleivably snappy and fast. I can't recommend Forgejo enough.

在 HN 中查看
CptKriechstrom 2026/04/28 02:43
感觉就像每次我需要在晚上花一些时间来完成工作时，github 都会知道并将混乱猴子调到十一点。
It feels like that every time I need to put in some evening hours to get things done, github knows and turns the chaos monkey up to eleven.
在 HN 中查看
arcza 2026/04/28 02:59
嗯...我的 gitlab.internal.corp.com 已经启动了。每天都是这样。每几个月的维护工作最多需要大约1小时。
Well... my gitlab.internal.corp.com is up. As it is every day. Takes about 1 hour every few months of maintenance work at most.
在 HN 中查看
SenHeng 2026/04/28 01:42
在过去一小时内，PR 或问题页面将加载 0 个项目。有时，会出现错误提示“无法获取数据”。
For the past hour, the PR or Issues pages would load with 0 items. Occasionally, there’s an error toast mentioning ‘failure to fetch data’.
在 HN 中查看
swiftcoder 2026/04/28 03:00
哦，比这更有趣。如果您坐在那里按 F5，迟早您会获得正确的页面加载。因此，服务器的一小部分正在出售正确的数据，而其余的则不是
Oh, it's even more fun than that. If you sit there hitting F5, sooner or later you will get a proper page load. So some small subset of the servers is vending the correct data, and the rest not
在 HN 中查看
erikbye 2026/04/28 03:11
制作了我自己的 github 替代方法。有点像类固醇的 cgit。我现在只使用github进行免费备份。
Made my own github alternative ways back. Kind of like cgit on steroids. I only use github for free backup now.

Releases aren't being published for me.

blurbleblurble 2026/04/28 02:43
也许这就是为什么我无法通过语言过滤我的星星

在 HN 中查看
hx8 2026/04/28 03:06
我的新项目不使用 GitHub，并且不会将 GitHub 用作镜像。 对于开发工具来说，两个九的可靠性是不够的。

GitHub 目前空间有限。 软件开发的步伐不断加快，它们处于承重的地位。 此外，他们的 GitHub Copilot 许可证造成了巨大的损失，不仅直接损失了他们的金钱，还使流量问题变得更加严重。 简而言之，他们并没有像当前情况那样优先考虑可扩展性和可靠性，而是专注于功能构建，这些功能可以归结为微软的人工智能中间人销售人员。

他们的位置很艰难，但他们可能会严重掉球。 我不相信他们在我想要进行生产部署之前不会停机。

My new projects do not use GitHub, and will not use GitHub as anything more than a mirror. Two nines of reliability isn't enough for devtools.

GitHub is in a tight space right now. The pace of software development is increasing and they are in a load-bearing position. In addition, their GitHub Copilot license was a massive loss-leader both directly costing them money, and making the traffic problem even worse. Simply put, they aren't prioritizing scaling and reliability like they need to be in this current situation and instead are focusing on feature build outs that boil down to being Microsoft's AI Middleman Salesperson.

Their position is hard, but they are potentially fumbling the ball in a big way. I for one don't trust them to not be down right before I want to do a production deploy.

在 HN 中查看
corvad 2026/04/28 02:46
现在这是每周都会发生的事情......
At this point it's a weekly occurrence...
在 HN 中查看
loloquwowndueo 2026/04/28 02:52
如果现在每周只进行一次，那么所有的可靠性工作一定会得到回报！哈哈
All that reliability work must have paid off if it’s only happening weekly these days! lol

m_a_t_t_8_6 2026/04/28 02:10
公关不会加载，问题也不会加载。对于开发工作流程几乎无法使用。我觉得很多人对 GH 可靠性的担忧有点戏剧化，但这似乎相当重要（至少对我来说），而且似乎没有得到那么多的报道。
Pr's don't load, issues don't load. Pretty much unusable for dev workflows. I felt like a lot of the hand wringing over GH reliability was a bit dramatic but this one seems pretty major (at least for me) and doesn't seem to even be getting that much coverage.

shevy-java 2026/04/28 03:31
微软最近的宣传很不好。首先是 GitHub，然后是 最近人工智能的坏消息（https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-04-27/microsoft...）。他们 正在真正地、慢慢地成为 Microslop Incorporated。

帝国可能会崩溃……

Microsoft is making bad publicity lately. First GitHub, then the bad recent AI news (https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-04-27/microsoft...). They are truly, slowly, becoming Microslop Incorporated.

The Empire may fall ...

在 HN 中查看
rvz 2026/04/28 02:21
我认为，在微软收购 GitHub 后，我们已经给了它足够的时间（五年多的时间）来解决问题。

现在它正被埋入地下。

此时他们的聊天机器人 Tay.ai、Zo 和 Copilot 正在破坏这个平台，而且没有 GitHub 的首席执行官可以抱怨这一点，所以现在使用 GitHub 根本没有任何意义。 （特别是 GitHub Actions）

现在是自我托管而不是“将所有内容集中到 GitHub”的时候了。 [0]

[0] https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=22867803

I think we have given GitHub enough time (more than half a decade) after Microsoft acquired it to sort itself out.

It is now being run into the ground.

At this point their chatbots Tay.ai, Zo, and Copilot are wrecking the platform and there is no CEO of GitHub to complain to about this so it now makes no sense to use GitHub at all. (Especially GitHub Actions)

It is now time to self host and not "centralize everything to GitHub". [0]

[0] https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=22867803

throwaway613746 2026/04/28 02:48 deleted

在 HN 中查看
sasham 2026/04/28 04:15
我们才刚刚开始看到人工智能对基础设施的影响——可能还不到即将发生的影响的 1%。今天的回购托管无法扩展 - 它需要从基础架构开始从头开始重建。我认为我们不应该回到自我托管并通过电子邮件共享补丁......

（FWIW https://diversion.dev 正常运行时间为 100%。显然，规模不同，但我们也不是微软。）

We're barely starting to see AI's impact on infra - probably <1% of what's coming. Repo hosting as it is today won't scale - it needs to be rebuilt from the ground up, starting with basic architecture. And I don't think we should go back to self hosting and sharing patches over email...

(FWIW https://diversion.dev is at 100% uptime. Different scale, obviously, but also we're not Microsoft.)

在 HN 中查看
ragall 2026/04/28 04:24
我建议使用 uBlock 测试您的网站（并启用其所有过滤器列表）。我所看到的只是一个几乎空白的页面。不要指向第三方 CDN 上的 JS 或 CSS，因为由于跨站共享的变化，Chrome 和 Firefox 都不会从缓存中受益。
I suggest testing your website with uBlock (and all its filter lists enabled). All I see is an almost empty page. Don't point to JS or CSS on third-party CDNs because due to the changes in cross-site sharing, neither Chrome nor Firefox will benefit from cacheing.
在 HN 中查看
sasham 2026/04/28 06:06
谢谢你的提示！刚刚做了，一切似乎都正常。也许 Webflow 出了问题？
Thanks for the tip! Just did, everything seems to work. Maybe Webflow had a glitch?
